title: Zelda-themed Gamified Multiplication App for 4th Graders

requirements.text: Design and apply a Zelda-inspired visual theme throughout the entire app: use a dark forest green primary color (#2D5A27), golden yellow for highlights and buttons (#C9A84C), parchment/tan for backgrounds (#F2E8C9), deep brown for borders and text (#3B1F0A), and red for danger/wrong answers (#8B0000). Use a fantasy serif or pixel-style font for headings. Add decorative Zelda-style UI elements such as bordered panels resembling HUD elements, heart icons for lives/health, and star/triforce-like accent shapes. Maintain this theme consistently across all screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary background uses parchment/tan (#F2E8C9) or dark green tones consistent with Zelda palette
Buttons and interactive elements use golden yellow (#C9A84C) styling
Fantasy-themed typography applied to headings and level titles
Decorative border/frame elements on panels and cards resemble Zelda HUD style
Heart or life icons visible in the UI
Color scheme is consistent on all screens

requirements.text: Create a Home/Start screen with a Zelda-styled title 'Quest of Numbers', a brief description ('Prove your multiplication mastery across 3 epic levels!'), and a 'Begin Quest' button that navigates to Level 1. Show locked/unlocked status for each of the 3 levels using Zelda-style icons (e.g., a sword icon for unlocked, a lock for locked).
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen displays the app title 'Quest of Numbers' in fantasy-style heading
Three level indicators are shown with locked/unlocked visual states
'Begin Quest' button navigates to Level 1
Screen is styled with Zelda color palette

requirements.text: Implement 3 progressive multiplication levels with increasing difficulty appropriate for a 4th grader. Level 1 (The Forest Trial): multiplication with single-digit numbers only (e.g., 3×4, 6×7, up to 9×9). Level 2 (The Temple Challenge): multiplication involving one factor up to 12 (e.g., 11×4, 12×7). Level 3 (The Final Dungeon): multiplication of two-digit numbers by single digits (e.g., 13×4, 15×6, 18×9). Each level presents 10 questions randomly generated. The player must answer 8 out of 10 correctly to pass and unlock the next level.
acceptanceCriteria: Level 1 generates random single-digit × single-digit multiplication questions
Level 2 generates random questions with at least one factor up to 12
Level 3 generates random questions involving a two-digit number multiplied by a single digit
Each level consists of exactly 10 questions
Player must score at least 8/10 to pass the level
Completing a level unlocks the next level
Questions are randomly generated each attempt

requirements.text: Build a gameplay screen for each level that shows: the level name with a Zelda-style title banner, a question counter (e.g., 'Question 3 of 10'), a health bar or heart icons (3 hearts, losing one per wrong answer after the 2nd mistake), the current multiplication question displayed prominently, a numeric input field and 'Submit' button, immediate visual/audio-style feedback (green glow + 'Correct!' message or red flash + 'Wrong!' message with the correct answer shown). Display a progress bar showing questions answered.
acceptanceCriteria: Level name and banner displayed at top of gameplay screen
Question counter (e.g., 'Question X of 10') is visible
Heart icons displayed representing player lives/health
Multiplication question is displayed prominently in large text
Numeric input field accepts player's answer
Submit button triggers answer evaluation
Correct answer triggers green visual feedback with 'Correct!' text
Wrong answer triggers red visual feedback with 'Wrong! The answer was X' text
Progress bar reflects answered question count

requirements.text: Implement a Level Complete screen shown after passing a level (score ≥ 8/10) with a Zelda-styled victory message (e.g., 'You obtained the Forest Medallion!'), the player's score, a 'Continue to Next Level' button (or 'Return to Map' if on Level 3). Implement a Level Failed screen shown when score < 8/10, with a Zelda-styled message (e.g., 'The dungeon defeated you...'), the player's score, and a 'Try Again' button to restart the level.
acceptanceCriteria: Level Complete screen shows victory message with Zelda-themed flavor text
Level Complete screen shows player score out of 10
'Continue to Next Level' or 'Return to Map' button is functional
Level Failed screen shows defeat message with Zelda-themed flavor text
Level Failed screen shows player score
'Try Again' button restarts the current level with new random questions

requirements.text: Persist level unlock progress in the backend. Store which levels are unlocked per anonymous session (or globally, since there's no auth). The backend should expose functions to get unlocked levels and to unlock the next level upon passing. The frontend reads unlock state on load and updates it when a level is passed.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores unlock state for levels 1, 2, and 3
Level 1 is always unlocked by default
Level 2 unlocks only after Level 1 is passed
Level 3 unlocks only after Level 2 is passed
Frontend reads unlock state from backend on app load
Frontend updates backend when a level is passed successfully
Locked levels are visually indicated as inaccessible on the home screen

requirements.text: Generate the following static image assets for the app: a pixel-art style parchment scroll background texture for panels, a triforce-inspired golden icon accent, and a Zelda-style game logo banner for 'Quest of Numbers'. Store all generated images in frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them throughout the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Parchment scroll background texture image is rendered in panel/card backgrounds
Triforce-inspired golden icon is rendered as an accent on the home screen
Game logo banner image is displayed on the home screen title area
All images are served from frontend/public/assets/generated

constraints: Only 3 levels must be implemented — no more, no less
Visual theme must strictly follow Zelda-inspired color palette (greens, golds, parchment, dark browns)
Multiplication difficulty must be appropriate for a 4th-grade level (no algebra, no division)
All backend logic must remain in the single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: User authentication or Internet Identity login
Division, addition, subtraction, or other math operations
Mobile-native features or native app builds

required: Pixel-art style parchment/aged paper scroll texture background in golden-tan tones with worn edges (parchment-bg.dim_800x600.png)
Triforce-shaped golden yellow geometric icon on a transparent background, pixel-art style (triforce-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Fantasy game title banner with the text 'Quest of Numbers' styled in a Zelda-like gold-on-dark-green engraved look (title-banner.dim_800x200.png)
